sections.markdown:
# Cisco ASA Firewall Log Analysis

This notebook contains DQL queries for analyzing Cisco Secure Firewall ASA syslog messages ingested by the **Cisco ASA Firewall Log Generator** Dynatrace workflow.

The workflow generates a realistic mix of connection events, ACL decisions, port scans, and VPN/AAA events. Logs are ingested into Grail with structured `cisco.*` attributes following the official ASA syslog message ID format (`%ASA-<severity>-<msgid>:`).

| Attribute | Values |
|---|---|
| `log.source` | `cisco-asa` |
| `cisco.message_id` | `106023`, `106100`, `302013`, `302014`, `302015`, `302016`, `710003`, `113039`, `113019`, `113005` |
| `cisco.action` | `deny`, `permit`, `built`, `teardown`, `auth-failed` |

---

## 1. All Cisco ASA Logs — Overview
All ingested syslog entries with key parsed fields. Use this as a starting point to confirm data is flowing.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| fields timestamp,
         cisco.message_id,
         cisco.action,
         cisco.src_ip,
         cisco.dst_ip,
         cisco.protocol,
         cisco.src_port,
         cisco.dst_port,
         cisco.acl_name,
         cisco.user,
         cisco.reason,
         hostname
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 2. Denied / Blocked Events
All access-list denials and authentication failures. Validates the firewall **IS** blocking traffic.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.action, "deny", "auth-failed")
| fields timestamp,
         cisco.message_id,
         cisco.action,
         cisco.src_ip,
         cisco.dst_ip,
         cisco.protocol,
         cisco.dst_port,
         cisco.acl_name,
         cisco.user
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 3. Permitted / Connected Events
All permitted or built connections — confirms the firewall is **NOT** blocking all traffic.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.action, "permit", "built")
| fields timestamp,
         cisco.message_id,
         cisco.action,
         cisco.src_ip,
         cisco.dst_ip,
         cisco.protocol,
         cisco.dst_port,
         cisco.acl_name,
         cisco.connection_id
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 4. Event Summary — Deny vs Permit
Single-glance validation: is the expected mix of allowed and blocked traffic present?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| fieldsAdd is_blocked = in(cisco.action, "deny", "auth-failed")
| summarize total = count(),
            denied = countIf(is_blocked),
            permitted = countIf(NOT is_blocked)
| fieldsAdd denied_pct = round(toDouble(denied) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1),
            permitted_pct = round(toDouble(permitted) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1)

sections.markdown:
## 5. Denied by ACL Name
Which access-list rules (msg 106023) are firing most? Key for understanding deny policy coverage.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter cisco.message_id == "106023"
| summarize deny_count = count(),
            unique_sources = countDistinct(cisco.src_ip),
            unique_ports = countDistinct(cisco.dst_port),
            by: { cisco.acl_name }
| sort deny_count desc

sections.markdown:
## 6. Denied by Destination Port
Which ports are being blocked most frequently? Helps identify scan activity or misconfigured services.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.action, "deny")
| summarize deny_count = count(),
            protocols = collectDistinct(cisco.protocol),
            acls_applied = collectDistinct(cisco.acl_name),
            by: { cisco.dst_port }
| sort deny_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 7. Events by Message ID
Volume distribution across all 10 ASA syslog message IDs. Each ID maps to a specific event class.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| summarize count = count(),
            by: { cisco.message_id }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 8. VPN Session Analysis
Tunnels started (113039), disconnected (113019), and authentication failures (113005) by VPN group.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.message_id, "113039", "113019", "113005")
| summarize total = count(),
            started = countIf(cisco.message_id == "113039"),
            disconnected = countIf(cisco.message_id == "113019"),
            auth_failed = countIf(cisco.message_id == "113005"),
            by: { cisco.group }
| sort total desc

sections.markdown:
## 9. Top Denied Source IPs
Which source IPs are generating the most denied traffic? Potential scanners or threat actors.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.action, "deny", "auth-failed")
| summarize deny_count = count(),
            targets = countDistinct(cisco.dst_ip),
            msg_ids = collectDistinct(cisco.message_id),
            by: { cisco.src_ip }
| sort deny_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 10. Top Targeted Destination IPs
Which internal IPs are receiving the most denied traffic? Potential high-value targets being probed.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.action, "deny")
| summarize deny_count = count(),
            attacking_sources = countDistinct(cisco.src_ip),
            ports_targeted = collectDistinct(cisco.dst_port),
            by: { cisco.dst_ip }
| sort deny_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 11. Security Events Over Time
Time series of security events (deny/auth-failed) vs normal events — use to spot anomaly spikes.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| fieldsAdd is_security = in(cisco.action, "deny", "auth-failed")
| makeTimeseries security_events = countIf(is_security),
                 normal_events = countIf(NOT is_security),
                 interval: 5m

sections.markdown:
## 12. High-Risk Port Blocks
Blocks on sensitive ports: RDP (3389), SQL Server (1433), SMB (445), Telnet (23), FTP (21), SSH (22).

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter in(cisco.action, "deny")
| filter cisco.dst_port == 3389
     OR cisco.dst_port == 1433
     OR cisco.dst_port == 445
     OR cisco.dst_port == 23
     OR cisco.dst_port == 21
     OR cisco.dst_port == 22
| fields timestamp,
         cisco.src_ip,
         cisco.dst_ip,
         cisco.dst_port,
         cisco.protocol,
         cisco.action,
         cisco.acl_name,
         cisco.message_id
| sort timestamp desc

sections.markdown:
## 13. Connection Teardown Reason Breakdown
Why are TCP/UDP connections ending? (msg 302014 — teardown). `TCP Reset` and `Flow closed by inspection` indicate security actions; `TCP FIN` is normal.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter cisco.message_id == "302014"
| summarize count = count(),
            by: { cisco.reason, cisco.protocol }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 14. VPN Disconnects by Reason and Group
Breakdown of VPN tunnel disconnects (msg 113019) by disconnect reason and VPN group. Helps identify unstable groups or forced disconnects.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter cisco.message_id == "113019"
| summarize count = count(),
            unique_users = countDistinct(cisco.user),
            by: { cisco.reason, cisco.group }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 15. AAA / Authentication Failure Analysis
All authentication failures (msg 113005) broken down by user and VPN group. High failure counts for a single user indicate credential stuffing or a locked-out user.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "cisco-asa"
| filter cisco.message_id == "113005"
| summarize auth_failure_count = count(),
            groups_attempted = collectDistinct(cisco.group),
            by: { cisco.user }
| sort auth_failure_count desc
| limit 20
